The NSW Government announced a $225 million investment in the 2026 NSW Budget to support critical grid upgrades. This will enable the extension of the 500 kV transmission network to Dinawan substation, unlocking 1.3 GW of network capacity. Procurement for early works contractors, an additional transformer, and other critical long-lead items like circuit breakers is underway, with early-stage construction at Dinawan substation commencing.
projects.statusNote: New funding and initiation of procurement for key transmission components and early works.

projects.summary: Stage 1 of the network infrastructure project is complete, unlocking 350MW of new capacity, with a broader plan to unlock 1GW by 2028 and connect an additional 1.8GW of renewable generation and storage projects. Construction started in March 2026.

projects.summary: The Illawarra REZ, NSW's first urban REZ, has an intended network capacity of 1 GW. EnergyCo is consulting with network planners to specify the network infrastructure. The REZ was formally declared in February 2023, and discussions in December 2025 mapped out next steps, including decarbonisation of local industry and utilising integrated consumer energy sources.
projects.statusNote: Ongoing planning and consultation for an existing REZ, with a focus on network infrastructure specification.
